items.situation: student talking to dorm RA about noise complaint

conversation.speaker: Casey
conversation.text: Hey Sarah, got a sec? I really need to talk about my neighbor.

conversation.speaker: Sarah
conversation.text: Sure, Casey. What's going on?

conversation.speaker: Casey
conversation.text: It's the music. Every night after midnight, it's so loud I can't sleep.

conversation.speaker: Sarah
conversation.text: Oh, that's not good. Have you tried talking to them directly?

conversation.speaker: Casey
conversation.text: Yeah, I knocked last night. They turned it down, but it was back up in an hour.

conversation.speaker: Sarah
conversation.text: Hmm, okay. For a formal complaint, I need the room number and specific times.

conversation.speaker: Casey
conversation.text: It's 305. And it's always like 12:30 to 2 AM.

conversation.speaker: Sarah
conversation.text: Got it. I'll speak to them today. If it continues, we can escalate.

conversation.speaker: Casey
conversation.text: Thanks, I appreciate it. I just need some quiet to study.

questions.question: What specific action has Casey already taken regarding the noise?

options.A: She filed a formal written complaint with the housing office.
options.B: She asked the RA to immediately confront the neighbor.
options.C: She spoke to the neighbor about the problem herself.
options.D: She recorded the noise to use as evidence.

questions.explanation: 凯西说：'我昨晚敲过门了。他们拒绝了……' 表明她已经直接和邻居谈过了。
questions.stem: What specific action has Casey already taken regarding the noise?

questions.question: What will Sarah most likely do immediately after this conversation?

options.A: Escalate the complaint to the campus police.
options.B: Visit room 305 to address the issue.
options.C: Ask Casey to gather more evidence over the weekend.
options.D: Schedule a meeting between Casey and the neighbor.

questions.explanation: 莎拉说：'我今天会和他们谈谈，' 暗示她的下一步是直接与305房间的邻居沟通。
questions.stem: What will Sarah most likely do immediately after this conversation?

items.situation: student checking in at campus health center

conversation.speaker: Avery
conversation.text: Hi, I have an appointment? It's at 10:30.

conversation.speaker: Jordan
conversation.text: Name, please?

conversation.speaker: Avery
conversation.text: Avery Jones.

conversation.speaker: Jordan
conversation.text: Okay, I see you. For Dr. Lee. You're a few minutes early, that's fine.

conversation.speaker: Avery
conversation.text: Great. Um, do I need to fill anything out? I've never been here before.

conversation.speaker: Jordan
conversation.text: Yes, just this new patient form. Here's a clipboard and pen.

conversation.speaker: Avery
conversation.text: Thanks. Also, will this be covered by the student insurance?

conversation.speaker: Jordan
conversation.text: For a routine visit, yes. If you need labs or meds, there might be a small copay.

conversation.speaker: Avery
conversation.text: Okay, that's a relief. I'll just fill this out then.

conversation.speaker: Jordan
conversation.text: Perfect. Have a seat, and we'll call you when the doctor's ready.

questions.question: Why does Jordan give Avery a clipboard?

options.A: To sign a consent form for a specific procedure.
options.B: To complete a new patient information form.
options.C: To write down a list of current symptoms.
options.D: To provide proof of student insurance coverage.

questions.explanation: 乔丹明确表示：'是的，就这份新患者表格。这是写字板和笔。'
questions.stem: Why does Jordan give Avery a clipboard?

questions.question: What can be inferred about Avery's visit?

options.A: Avery is running late for a very important appointment.
options.B: Avery is concerned about the potential cost of the visit.
options.C: Avery is seeing a specialist for a serious condition.
options.D: Avery has visited this health center many times before.

questions.explanation: 艾弗里询问就诊是否在保险范围内，得知肯定答复后松了口气，暗示费用曾是她的顾虑。
questions.stem: What can be inferred about Avery's visit?

items.situation: student asking admin staff about registration

conversation.speaker: Rachel
conversation.text: Excuse me, I'm trying to register for Chemistry 101, but the portal says it's closed.

conversation.speaker: Casey
conversation.text: Let me check... Hmm, it looks full. There's a waitlist, though.

conversation.speaker: Rachel
conversation.text: A waitlist? How does that work?

conversation.speaker: Casey
conversation.text: You add yourself online. If a spot opens, you get an email and have 24 hours to register.

conversation.speaker: Rachel
conversation.text: Oh, okay. What are my chances? I really need this class for my major.

conversation.speaker: Casey
conversation.text: Pretty good, actually. A lot of students drop in the first week. Just check your email constantly.

conversation.speaker: Rachel
conversation.text: I will. And if I don't get in...?

conversation.speaker: Casey
conversation.text: Then come see me. We can look at alternative courses or a permission number from the professor.

conversation.speaker: Rachel
conversation.text: Alright, thanks for your help. I'll go get on that list now.

questions.question: According to Casey, what must Rachel do if a spot in the class opens?

options.A: Call the admin office within one business day.
options.B: Register for the class within 24 hours of notification.
options.C: Meet with the professor to get a permission number.
options.D: Immediately pay the tuition for the course.

questions.explanation: 凯西说：'如果有空位，你会收到邮件，并有24小时注册。'
questions.stem: According to Casey, what must Rachel do if a spot in the class opens?

questions.question: What does Casey imply about the first week of the chemistry class?

options.A: It is when the most difficult material is covered.
options.B: It is when many students decide to drop the course.
options.C: It is the only time to join the class waitlist.
options.D: It is when the professor distributes permission numbers.

questions.explanation: 凯西表示：'很多学生第一周会退课，' 暗示这就是为什么瑞秋在候补名单上机会很大的原因。
questions.stem: What does Casey imply about the first week of the chemistry class?

items.situation: student discussing lease renewal with landlord

conversation.speaker: Taylor
conversation.text: Hi James, I got your email about renewing the lease. I'd like to stay another year.

conversation.speaker: James
conversation.text: Great, Taylor. You've been a good tenant. The new lease is ready, but there's a small rent increase.

conversation.speaker: Taylor
conversation.text: An increase? How much are we talking?

conversation.speaker: James
conversation.text: Just fifty dollars a month. It's standard, covers rising property taxes.

conversation.speaker: Taylor
conversation.text: Fifty... Okay, I guess that's manageable. Does it include anything new, like parking?

conversation.speaker: James
conversation.text: Parking's still extra, I'm afraid. But I did have the HVAC serviced, so you shouldn't have AC issues again.

conversation.speaker: Taylor
conversation.text: That's a plus. Last summer was rough. Can I sign the new lease tomorrow?

conversation.speaker: James
conversation.text: Sure thing. Stop by the office after noon. I'll have it printed.

conversation.speaker: Taylor
conversation.text: Perfect. See you then.

questions.question: What reason does James give for the rent increase?

options.A: To pay for a new parking area for tenants.
options.B: To cover increased property tax costs.
options.C: To fund major renovations to the building.
options.D: To compensate for Taylor's past late payments.

questions.explanation: 詹姆斯明确说明涨价是'标准操作，用于覆盖上涨的房产税。'
questions.stem: What reason does James give for the rent increase?

questions.question: What problem did Taylor likely experience in the previous year?

options.A: Difficulty finding parking near the apartment.
options.B: Issues with the air conditioning system.
options.C: Frequent disagreements with the landlord.
options.D: Problems paying the rent on time.

questions.explanation: 泰勒提到'去年夏天很艰难，' 詹姆斯接话说他检修了空调系统，所以泰勒'应该不会再遇到空调问题了。'
questions.stem: What problem did Taylor likely experience in the previous year?

items.situation: friends planning a weekend trip

conversation.speaker: Alex
conversation.text: So, for the trip this weekend... I was thinking we could camp at Lake Serene.

conversation.speaker: Morgan
conversation.text: Camping? I don't know. The forecast says it might rain.

conversation.speaker: Alex
conversation.text: Oh, I didn't check. Rain would be a bummer. We could get a cabin instead?

conversation.speaker: Morgan
conversation.text: Cabins are pricey, though. What about just a day hike? We drive up, hike, and come back.

conversation.speaker: Alex
conversation.text: That's less fun. I wanted to get away from campus overnight.

conversation.speaker: Morgan
conversation.text: Okay, how about this: we camp, but we also book a cheap motel as a backup? If it pours, we bail.

conversation.speaker: Alex
conversation.text: That's smart! I can look for a refundable motel room near the lake.

conversation.speaker: Morgan
conversation.text: Perfect. And I'll double-check the gear to make sure our tent is waterproof.

conversation.speaker: Alex
conversation.text: Deal. Let's hope for clear skies, but have a plan B.

questions.question: What is Morgan's initial concern about Alex's camping idea?

options.A: Camping gear is too expensive to rent.
options.B: The weather might be bad.
options.C: The lake is too far to drive to.
options.D: Camping permits are difficult to obtain.

questions.explanation: 摩根对露营提议回应道：'天气预报说可能会下雨。'
questions.stem: What is Morgan's initial concern about Alex's camping idea?

questions.question: What do the friends finally decide to do?

options.A: Cancel the trip entirely due to the weather risk.
options.B: Go camping but have a motel reservation as a backup.
options.C: Stay in a cabin regardless of the cost.
options.D: Take a day hike instead of an overnight trip.

questions.explanation: 他们同意了摩根的计划：'我们去露营，但也预订一个便宜的汽车旅馆作为后备。' 亚历克斯同意寻找可退款的房间。
questions.stem: What do the friends finally decide to do?

items.situation: student calling IT help desk about login issue

conversation.speaker: Maria
conversation.text: IT help desk, this is Maria.

conversation.speaker: Taylor
conversation.text: Hi, I can't log into the student portal. It says my password's invalid.

conversation.speaker: Maria
conversation.text: Okay, let's start with the basics. Are you sure you're using your full student email?

conversation.speaker: Taylor
conversation.text: Yeah, taylor.jones@university.edu. I just changed the password yesterday, actually.

conversation.speaker: Maria
conversation.text: Ah, that might be it. Sometimes the system takes a few hours to update. Did you get a confirmation email?

conversation.speaker: Taylor
conversation.text: I think so... Wait, no, I don't see one.

conversation.speaker: Maria
conversation.text: Then the change probably didn't go through. I can reset it for you right now. You'll get a temporary password.

conversation.speaker: Taylor
conversation.text: That would be great. I need to register for classes today.

conversation.speaker: Maria
conversation.text: No problem. Check your email in two minutes. Use the temp password, then create a new one immediately.

conversation.speaker: Taylor
conversation.text: Will do. Thanks for your help, Maria.

questions.question: Why does Maria think Taylor's password change might not have worked?

options.A: Taylor did not use a complex enough password.
options.B: The system update can sometimes be delayed.
options.C: Taylor likely entered the wrong student email address.
options.D: Password changes are not allowed right before registration.

questions.explanation: 玛丽亚说：'系统有时需要几个小时更新。'
questions.stem: Why does Maria think Taylor's password change might not have worked?

questions.question: What will Taylor need to do immediately after using the temporary password?

options.A: Call the help desk again to confirm the login worked.
options.B: Wait 24 hours before attempting to log in again.
options.C: Create a new permanent password.
options.D: Check for a confirmation email from the day before.

questions.explanation: 玛丽亚的指示是：'使用临时密码，然后立即创建新密码。'
questions.stem: What will Taylor need to do immediately after using the temporary password?

items.situation: roommates discussing apartment chores

conversation.speaker: David
conversation.text: Hey Lisa, we need to talk about the kitchen. The sink's been full of dishes for two days.

conversation.speaker: Lisa
conversation.text: I know, I'm sorry. I had that huge biology midterm. I'll do them tonight, I promise.

conversation.speaker: David
conversation.text: It's just... we agreed. Whoever cooks, the other cleans. I cooked last night.

conversation.speaker: Lisa
conversation.text: You're right, you're right. I dropped the ball. Tonight, I'll clean everything, even the pans.

conversation.speaker: David
conversation.text: Thanks. And look, maybe our system isn't working during exam weeks. What if we just alternate days?

conversation.speaker: Lisa
conversation.text: That could be simpler. Like, I'm responsible for Mondays, Wednesdays, Fridays?

conversation.speaker: David
conversation.text: Yeah, and I'll take the other days. That way it's clear, even when we're busy.

conversation.speaker: Lisa
conversation.text: Deal. And again, sorry about the mess. I'll get it handled right after class.

conversation.speaker: David
conversation.text: Okay, cool. Good luck on your midterm, by the way.

questions.question: What was the roommates' original agreement about kitchen chores?

options.A: They would hire a cleaning service every other week.
options.B: The person who did not cook would clean the dishes.
options.C: They would each clean their own dishes immediately.
options.D: They would alternate full cleaning days each week.

questions.explanation: 大卫说：'我们约定好了。谁做饭，另一个人就洗碗。'
questions.stem: What was the roommates' original agreement about kitchen chores?

questions.question: Why does David suggest changing the chore system?

options.A: He believes Lisa is intentionally avoiding her chores.
options.B: He thinks the current system fails during busy academic periods.
options.C: He wants to take on more chores to help Lisa study.
options.D: He is planning to cook less often in the future.

questions.explanation: 大卫说：'也许我们的系统在考试周失灵了，' 暗示系统在他们忙于学业时会出问题。
questions.stem: Why does David suggest changing the chore system?

items.situation: student checking with mail room about a missing package

conversation.speaker: Laura
conversation.text: Hi, I'm hoping you can help me. I got a notification that a package was delivered to the mail room yesterday, but I can't find it in my locker.

conversation.speaker: Kevin
conversation.text: Oh no! That's not good. Did you get a text with the locker combination?

conversation.speaker: Laura
conversation.text: Yeah, I did. But when I went to locker 42, it was completely empty.

conversation.speaker: Kevin
conversation.text: Hmm, okay. Actually, we've had a few issues with the new automated system. Sometimes it assigns a locker but the notification gets delayed.

conversation.speaker: Laura
conversation.text: Really? So my package might still be here?

conversation.speaker: Kevin
conversation.text: It's possible. Let me check the back room. We hold oversized items there if they don't fit in the lockers. What's the tracking number?

conversation.speaker: Laura
conversation.text: It's on my phone... here, it's 9Z-781-445.

conversation.speaker: Kevin
conversation.text: Got it. I'll be right back.

conversation.speaker: Kevin
conversation.text: Good news! It's right here. It's a large box, so it didn't go to a locker. Sorry about the confusion.

conversation.speaker: Laura
conversation.text: Whew, that's a relief! Thanks so much. I'll definitely double-check the back room next time.

questions.stem: Why does Laura approach Kevin?

options.A: To report that she cannot locate a package she was notified about
options.B: To complain about a broken locker in the mail room
options.C: To ask how to sign up for delivery notifications
options.D: To return an incorrectly delivered package

questions.explanation: 劳拉直接陈述了她的问题：她收到了包裹送达通知，但发现指定的储物柜是空的。B是错误话题；未提及储物柜损坏。C是相关但未陈述的行动；未讨论注册通知。D是反向情况；她是去取包裹，而不是退货。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the mail room's procedure?

options.A: Students must show ID to collect any package.
options.B: The staff prefers students to use an online contact form.
options.C: Oversized packages are handled differently than standard ones.
options.D: The automated locker system is completely reliable.

questions.explanation: 凯文解释说超大件物品存放在后室而非储物柜，表明流程不同。A是相关但未陈述的细节；未提及出示身份证。B是不相关的下一步；未讨论在线表格。D是反向情况；对话提到系统问题，暗示它并不完全可靠。

items.situation: two students talking about a new vending machine or campus facility

conversation.speaker: Jessica
conversation.text: Hey Eric, have you tried the new hydration station next to the gym? I just filled my bottle there.

conversation.speaker: Eric
conversation.text: The what? You mean a water fountain?

conversation.speaker: Jessica
conversation.text: Well, it's way more than that. It's this high-tech machine that dispenses chilled, filtered water, but you can also add electrolytes or vitamins with a tap. It's free for students.

conversation.speaker: Eric
conversation.text: Wait, seriously? That sounds great. I'm always buying sports drinks after my workouts.

conversation.speaker: Jessica
conversation.text: Exactly! You can customize it. I had the lemon-electrolyte mix today. It was perfect.

conversation.speaker: Eric
conversation.text: Huh. I've been going to the vending machine in the lobby for a Gatorade. That's like three dollars a pop.

conversation.speaker: Jessica
conversation.text: I know, right? The station's been there for a week, but there's no sign or anything. I only found it because someone in my yoga class mentioned it.

conversation.speaker: Eric
conversation.text: Good call telling me. I'll definitely check it out after my session today. Does it get crowded?

conversation.speaker: Jessica
conversation.text: Not yet, but I bet it will once word gets out. You should bring your own bottle, though. It doesn't provide cups.

conversation.speaker: Eric
conversation.text: Got it. Thanks for the tip! I'm gonna save so much money.

questions.stem: What is the main topic of the conversation?

options.A: A problem with the gym's vending machine prices
options.B: A new campus resource that one student is informing the other about
options.C: Planning a joint workout session at the campus gym
options.D: Comparing the benefits of different types of sports drinks

questions.explanation: 杰西卡向不知情的埃里克介绍并描述了新的饮水站。A是错误话题；虽然提到自动售货机价格，但这不是核心问题。C是错误话题；未讨论锻炼计划。D是过于狭隘的细节；比较饮料是讨论的一部分，但不是主要焦点，焦点是新设施本身。

questions.stem: What does Eric imply about his past habit?

options.A: He often forgot to bring a water bottle to the gym.
options.B: He was unaware of healthier drink alternatives.
options.C: He preferred the taste of brand-name sports drinks.
options.D: He spent more money than necessary on post-workout drinks.

questions.explanation: 埃里克说他一直在买三美元的运动饮料，并总结说使用免费饮水站将'省下很多钱'，暗示他过去的习惯是不必要的开销。A是相关但未陈述的细节；未讨论忘记带水杯。B是过度推断；对话未涉及健康意识。C是相关但未陈述的细节；未提及口味偏好。

items.situation: student reporting an issue with self-checkout machine

conversation.speaker: Ashley
conversation.text: Excuse me, the self-checkout machine isn't working for me. It won't scan my book.

conversation.speaker: James
conversation.text: Okay, let's see. Did it show an error message?

conversation.speaker: Ashley
conversation.text: Yeah, it says 'Item not recognized.' But it's definitely a library book. Look, here's the barcode.

conversation.speaker: James
conversation.text: Hmm. Actually, sometimes the barcode gets worn out. I can check it out for you manually at the main desk.

conversation.speaker: Ashley
conversation.text: Oh, that'd be great. I'm in a bit of a rush.

conversation.speaker: James
conversation.text: No worries. Just bring it over here. I'll need your student ID.

conversation.speaker: Ashley
conversation.text: Sure, here it is. So, does this happen a lot?

conversation.speaker: James
conversation.text: With older books, yeah. The sun from the windows fades the barcodes. We're replacing them gradually.

conversation.speaker: Ashley
conversation.text: I see. Well, thanks for your help!

conversation.speaker: James
conversation.text: You're welcome. You're all set. The book's due in three weeks.

questions.stem: What problem does Ashley report?

options.A: The library book she wants is not on the shelf.
options.B: Her student ID card is not scanning properly.
options.C: The self-checkout machine cannot read her book's barcode.
options.D: The main desk is closed and she cannot check out her book.

questions.explanation: 阿什利说机器'扫不了我的书'，并显示'物品无法识别'错误。A是错误细节；她手里拿着书。B是归属错误的细节；后来要求出示学生证，但这不是最初的问题。D是反向情况；主服务台是开放的，詹姆斯提出在那里提供帮助。

questions.stem: What will Ashley most likely do the next time she has a similar problem?

options.A: Go directly to the main desk for assistance.
options.B: Try using a different self-checkout machine.
options.C: Report the faded barcode to the library director.
options.D: Avoid checking out older books from the library.

questions.explanation: 詹姆斯通过在主服务台手动借出这本书解决了她当前的问题，提供了一个她可能会再次使用的成功解决方案。B是被拒绝的选项；她试过一台机器，但隐含原因（条形码褪色）在任何机器上都可能持续存在。C是过度推断；向主管报告过于极端且未被建议。D是不相关的下一步；未讨论或暗示避免借阅旧书。

items.situation: A student inquires about new quiet study pods but learns they are not yet available for general booking.

conversation.speaker: Sarah
conversation.text: Hi, I heard you installed those new, super-quiet study pods. I'd love to book one for my online exam next week.

conversation.speaker: David
conversation.text: Oh, I'm afraid they're not quite ready for student reservations yet.

conversation.speaker: Sarah
conversation.text: Really? But the campus newsletter said they were launching this week.

conversation.speaker: David
conversation.text: Right, the hardware is installed, but the booking software's still being tested. We're hoping to have it live by Friday.

conversation.speaker: Sarah
conversation.text: Hmm, my exam is on Monday. Is there any way to get on a waitlist or something?

conversation.speaker: David
conversation.text: Actually, there is a pilot program. You can sign up at the front desk to be a beta tester. If you're selected, you'd get early access and provide feedback.

conversation.speaker: Sarah
conversation.text: A beta tester? That sounds interesting. What does the feedback involve?

conversation.speaker: David
conversation.text: Just a short online survey about your experience with the pod and the app. It'd help us work out any bugs before the full launch.

conversation.speaker: Sarah
conversation.text: Okay, I'll definitely sign up for that. Where's the form?

conversation.speaker: David
conversation.text: Just ask Julie at the circulation desk. She's managing the sign-up list. Good luck with your exam!

questions.stem: Why does Sarah approach David?

options.A: To inquire about booking a new type of study space
options.B: To report a problem with the library's newsletter
options.C: To ask for help finding a book for her exam
options.D: To volunteer for a library event next week

questions.explanation: 莎拉的开场白是关于为考试预订新的学习舱，这是核心话题。B是错误话题；通讯被提及，但报告其问题并非她的目的。C是错误话题；找书未被讨论。D是错误话题；志愿活动未被提及。

questions.stem: What will Sarah most likely do next?

options.A: Wait until Friday to book a pod online
options.B: Complain to the university about the delay
options.C: Go to the circulation desk to sign up
options.D: Look for a different library to take her exam

questions.explanation: 大卫告诉莎拉去借阅台找朱莉报名测试员计划，莎拉同意（'我肯定会报名'）。A是排除选项；她不会等待，因为她周一需要学习舱，且正在寻求测试员选项。B是过度推断；没有迹象表明她会投诉。D是无关后续步骤；寻找其他图书馆未被讨论。

items.situation: Two friends discuss a new policy that limits guest parking passes on weekends.

conversation.speaker: Emily
conversation.text: Hey Brian, did you see the email about weekend parking? My parents are visiting tomorrow.

conversation.speaker: Brian
conversation.text: Oh no, the one from campus security? Yeah, it's a new rule.

conversation.speaker: Emily
conversation.text: Right! It says each student can only get one free guest pass per month now. Last semester it was unlimited.

conversation.speaker: Brian
conversation.text: I know, it's because of all the construction. They've closed like half the lots.

conversation.speaker: Emily
conversation.text: Wow, that's a huge change. My brother was going to visit next weekend too. What am I supposed to tell him?

conversation.speaker: Brian
conversation.text: Well, you could have him park at the shopping center down the road and walk. It's only a ten-minute walk from here.

conversation.speaker: Emily
conversation.text: Hmm, that's not ideal with all his luggage. Is there any way to get a second pass?

conversation.speaker: Brian
conversation.text: Actually, the email said you can buy extra daily passes for ten dollars online. It's not free, but it's an option.

conversation.speaker: Emily
conversation.text: Okay, ten dollars isn't too bad. I'll check the website. Thanks for the tip!

conversation.speaker: Brian
conversation.text: No worries. Good luck with your parents!

questions.stem: What is the new parking policy?

options.A: All weekend parking is now free for guests
options.B: Students get one free guest pass per month
options.C: Guest parking is banned on weekends due to construction
options.D: Students must pay twenty dollars for a weekend pass

questions.explanation: 艾米丽明确说明新规定：'现在每位学生每月只能获得一张免费访客通行证。'A是相反情况；限制更严格，而非更自由。C是错误细节；停车未被禁止，只是受限。D是错误细节；额外通行证的费用是十美元，而非二十美元。

questions.stem: What will Emily most likely do about her brother's visit?

options.A: Tell him he cannot visit at all
options.B: Ask Brian to use his free pass for her
options.C: Have him park at the shopping center
options.D: Buy an extra parking pass online

questions.explanation: 艾米丽在将免费通行证用于父母后，需要为下周末的弟弟申请通行证。布莱恩提到可以在线购买额外通行证，费用十美元，艾米丽表示会'查看网站'，暗示这可能是她的计划。A是过度推断；她在寻找解决方案，而非取消。B是无关后续步骤；共享通行证未被讨论。C是排除选项；布莱恩建议了，但艾米丽表示因行李问题'不太理想'，可能性较低。

items.situation: A student tries to return a malfunctioning desk lamp at the campus store.

conversation.speaker: Karen
conversation.text: Hi, I'd like to return this lamp. I bought it last week, but the dimmer switch doesn't work at all.

conversation.speaker: Chris
conversation.text: Okay, do you have your receipt with you?

conversation.speaker: Karen
conversation.text: I don't, actually. I think I threw it away. But it's got the store's price tag right here.

conversation.speaker: Chris
conversation.text: Hmm, without a receipt, I can't process a refund. Store policy.

conversation.speaker: Karen
conversation.text: Seriously? Even if it's defective? I just want one that works.

conversation.speaker: Chris
conversation.text: I can offer you an even exchange for the same model. We'll just swap it out right now.

conversation.speaker: Karen
conversation.text: Oh, that would be perfect! Are you sure the new one will work okay?

conversation.speaker: Chris
conversation.text: We can test it at the counter before you leave. If there's a problem, bring it right back.

conversation.speaker: Karen
conversation.text: Sounds great. Thanks for being so helpful!

conversation.speaker: Chris
conversation.text: No problem at all. I'll go grab a new one from the stockroom.

questions.stem: Why is Karen unable to get a refund for the lamp?

options.A: The lamp is more than a week old
options.B: The price tag is missing from the lamp
options.C: She does not have the sales receipt
options.D: The store does not sell that model anymore

questions.explanation: 克里斯说：'没有收据，我无法办理退款。'A是错误细节；她'上周'购买，在典型退货期内，原因在于收据而非时间。B是相反情况；她指出价格标签还在。D是相关但未提及；未提及型号已停产。

questions.stem: What will Karen most likely do before leaving the store?

options.A: Test the replacement lamp at the counter
options.B: Look for her original receipt at home
options.C: Decide to keep the broken lamp
options.D: File a complaint with the store manager

questions.explanation: 克里斯提出在柜台测试新台灯，凯伦同意换货，这是合理的后续步骤。B是排除选项；她已说扔掉了收据，且问题通过换货解决。C是相反情况；她将获得替换品，而非保留损坏品。D是过度推断；她对解决方案满意并感谢克里斯，因此投诉可能性低。

items.situation: two students discussing an issue with a campus sustainability program

conversation.speaker: Ashley
conversation.text: Hey Mark, have you tried using the new campus compost bins? I just got a warning sticker on my dorm room door.

conversation.speaker: Mark
conversation.text: Oh no! Seriously? I haven't, but I've seen them around. What's the problem?

conversation.speaker: Ashley
conversation.text: Well, I put my coffee grounds and a banana peel in one yesterday, but apparently you can't use the regular plastic bags. It has to be a certified compostable bag.

conversation.speaker: Mark
conversation.text: Huh, I didn't know that. Where do you even get those special bags?

conversation.speaker: Ashley
conversation.text: That's the thing! The sustainability office gives out a free roll at the start of the semester, but I must've missed it. Now I have to go to the campus store to buy some.

conversation.speaker: Mark
conversation.text: That's annoying. But wait, couldn't you just dump the food scraps directly into the bin without a bag?

conversation.speaker: Ashley
conversation.text: Actually, you're right. The sticker says that's totally fine. I guess I was just on autopilot, grabbing a bag.

conversation.speaker: Mark
conversation.text: Good call. I'll remember that too. No bag, no problem.

questions.stem: Why does Ashley talk to Mark?

options.A: To discuss a problem she encountered with a campus program
options.B: To invite him to a sustainability office event
options.C: To ask where to buy plastic bags for her dorm
options.D: To complain about the location of the compost bins

questions.explanation: 阿什利以收到堆肥桶警告贴纸开启对话，并讨论具体问题（使用了错误的袋子）。B是错误话题（未提及活动）。C是错误细节（她需要可堆肥袋，而非塑料袋）。D是相关但未提及（桶的位置未讨论）。

questions.stem: What will Ashley most likely do the next time she uses the compost bin?

options.A: Go to the campus store to buy the correct bags
options.B: Ignore the compost program entirely
options.C: Put her food scraps in without using a bag
options.D: Apply for a new roll of bags from the sustainability office

questions.explanation: 阿什利同意马克直接倾倒残渣的建议，马克总结'不用袋子，就没问题'，暗示这是接受的解决方案。A是排除选项（她了解到无需购买袋子）。B是过度推断（无证据表明她会退出计划）。D是无关后续步骤（获取新卷袋未作为此认知后的下一步讨论）。

items.situation: student checking with a receptionist about an appointment

conversation.speaker: Megan
conversation.text: Hi, I'm here for my 3:30 appointment with Dr. Evans? I'm a few minutes early.

conversation.speaker: Tom
conversation.text: Okay, let me check... Hmm, I don't see a Megan on the schedule for today with Dr. Evans.

conversation.speaker: Megan
conversation.text: What? That can't be right. I booked it online last week for career counseling.

conversation.speaker: Tom
conversation.text: Oh, wait. Dr. Evans is out of the office this afternoon. All his appointments were automatically rescheduled. Didn't you get a text message?

conversation.speaker: Megan
conversation.text: A text? I don't think so. I always check my email for confirmations.

conversation.speaker: Tom
conversation.text: Ah, that's it. Our system sends reschedule alerts via text only. Your new time is tomorrow at 11 AM. I can print you a confirmation slip right now.

conversation.speaker: Megan
conversation.text: Tomorrow at 11? Actually, I have a class then. Is there anything later in the week?

conversation.speaker: Tom
conversation.text: Let's see... How about Friday at 2 PM? He has an opening.

conversation.speaker: Megan
conversation.text: That works perfectly. Thanks for sorting that out!

questions.stem: Why was Megan's original appointment not on the schedule?

options.A: She accidentally booked it for the wrong month.
options.B: The doctor was out, and it had been rescheduled.
options.C: She did not confirm the appointment by email.
options.D: The receptionist could not find her name in the system.

questions.explanation: 汤姆说：'埃文斯博士今天下午不在办公室。他的所有预约已自动重新安排。'A是错误细节（未提及错误月份）。C是相关但未提及（邮件用于确认，而非重新安排的原因）。D是归因错误（他找不到预约是因为被重新安排，而非核心原因）。

questions.stem: What will Megan probably do to avoid this problem in the future?

options.A: Call the office instead of booking online
options.B: Arrive much earlier for all appointments
options.C: Double-check the doctor's schedule before booking
options.D: Pay more attention to text messages from the office

questions.explanation: 问题源于系统仅通过短信发送重新安排提醒，而梅根不查看短信。合理推断她将开始关注短信。A是过度推断（她成功在线预订；问题在于通知）。B是无关后续步骤（到达时间非问题所在）。C是排除选项（日程变更出乎意料，她无法提前查看）。

items.situation: student returning a defective item at the campus store

conversation.speaker: Maria
conversation.text: Hi, I'd like to return this desk lamp. I just bought it two days ago, and it flickers constantly.

conversation.speaker: Brian
conversation.text: Sure, do you have the receipt with you?

conversation.speaker: Maria
conversation.text: I don't, actually. I paid cash and must've tossed it. But it's your store brand, and I can show you the charge on my bank app.

conversation.speaker: Brian
conversation.text: Hmm, without a receipt, our policy is store credit only. But for defective electronics, we usually make an exception if it's within a week.

conversation.speaker: Maria
conversation.text: So I could get a refund back to my card?

conversation.speaker: Brian
conversation.text: Well, since you paid cash, we'd have to issue the refund in cash. But I'll need a manager's approval for that. She's on her break for another fifteen minutes.

conversation.speaker: Maria
conversation.text: I can wait, that's fine. I'd really prefer not to have store credit.

conversation.speaker: Brian
conversation.text: Okay, I'll put a note here. If you want to look around, I'll call you over when she's back.

questions.stem: What is Maria's main purpose for speaking with Brian?

options.A: To exchange a lamp for a different model
options.B: To find out the store's hours for returns
options.C: To get a refund for a malfunctioning product
options.D: To complain about the store's payment policy

questions.explanation: 玛丽亚表示她想退回有缺陷的台灯并寻求退款，这是核心问题。A过于狭隘（换货未讨论；她想要退货/退款）。B是错误话题（营业时间未提及）。D是相反情况（她讨论了政策，但主要目的并非投诉政策）。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the store's return policy?

options.A: It is more flexible for defective items than for other returns.
options.B: It requires all returns to be approved by a manager.
options.C: It does not allow returns without the original packaging.
options.D: It only offers exchanges, not refunds, for cash purchases.

questions.explanation: 布莱恩说无收据时政策是提供商店积分，'但对于有缺陷的电子产品，我们通常破例'，暗示更具灵活性。B是过度推断（经理批准仅针对此特定现金/例外情况，而非'所有退货'）。C是相关但未提及（包装未提及）。D是错误细节（经批准可能获得现金退款）。

items.situation: friends discussing an unexpected campus policy change

conversation.speaker: Amy
conversation.text: Nathan, did you see the email about the laundry rooms? They're changing how we pay.

conversation.speaker: Nathan
conversation.text: No, what's happening? I still have a bunch of those old tokens.

conversation.speaker: Amy
conversation.text: Exactly! That's the problem. Starting next Monday, they won't take tokens anymore. It's all through an app on your phone.

conversation.speaker: Nathan
conversation.text: You're kidding. I just bought ten tokens last week! What do we do with the old ones?

conversation.speaker: Amy
conversation.text: The email says you can exchange them for app credit at the housing office, but only until the end of the month.

conversation.speaker: Nathan
conversation.text: Whew, okay. I'll have to do that this week then. I hope the app is easy to use.

conversation.speaker: Amy
conversation.text: Me too. At least we won't have to hunt for quarters anymore!

questions.stem: What is the new policy for using the laundry rooms?

options.A: Payment must be made through a mobile application.
options.B: The cost per load is being reduced.
options.C: Tokens can only be used on certain days.
options.D: Students must pay with quarters instead of tokens.

questions.explanation: 艾米说：'从下周一始，他们将不再接受代币。全部通过手机应用进行。'B是相关但未提及（费用变更未提及）。C是错误细节（代币将完全不被接受）。D是相反情况（他们正在远离硬币等实体货币）。

questions.stem: What will Nathan probably do before the end of the month?

options.A: Use all his remaining tokens quickly
options.B: Complain to the housing office about the change
options.C: Exchange his tokens for credit at the housing office
options.D: Download the app and test it with one load

questions.explanation: 内森说他刚买了代币，得知可兑换后表示，'我这周得去办。'这直接暗示他将前往住宿办公室。A是排除选项（代币周一后将失效，他无法直接使用）。B是过度推断（他表示惊讶，但无投诉意图）。D是无关后续步骤（测试应用与他关于代币的即时关切无关）。

items.situation: student reporting an issue with a self-checkout machine

conversation.speaker: Katie
conversation.text: Hi, I'm having a weird problem with the self-checkout machine over there.

conversation.speaker: Alex
conversation.text: Oh no! What's going on?

conversation.speaker: Katie
conversation.text: Well, I scanned my student ID and it accepted it, but when I put my books on the pad, it charged me for five books instead of the two I'm actually checking out.

conversation.speaker: Alex
conversation.text: Hmm, that's odd. Did you have any other items in your bag on the scale?

conversation.speaker: Katie
conversation.text: Actually, yeah, I did. My heavy water bottle was still in there. Wait, seriously? Does the scale weigh everything?

conversation.speaker: Alex
conversation.text: Exactly. The system uses the weight to verify the number of items. It must've thought your bottle was three extra books.

conversation.speaker: Katie
conversation.text: Wow, I had no idea. So what should I do now? The receipt says I owe for five.

conversation.speaker: Alex
conversation.text: No worries. I can void that transaction at the main desk. Just bring your books and ID over, and we'll do a manual check-out. And next time, make sure the scale is clear.

conversation.speaker: Katie
conversation.text: Okay, that's a huge relief. I'll definitely do that. Thanks!

questions.stem: Why does Katie approach Alex?

options.A: To report an incorrect charge from a self-checkout machine
options.B: To ask how to use the library's new self-checkout system
options.C: To complain that a library book is missing from the shelves
options.D: To request help finding a specific book for her class

questions.explanation: 凯蒂的问题是自助借阅机向她收取了五本书而非两本的费用，她向亚历克斯报告了此事。B是错误话题（她知道如何使用，但遇到了错误）。C是错误话题（问题在于借阅，而非找书）。D是错误话题（她正在借书，而非找书）。

questions.stem: What will Katie most likely do the next time she uses the self-checkout machine?

options.A: Avoid using it and always go to the main desk
options.B: Make sure her student ID is properly scanned first
options.C: Remove all other items from the scale before weighing her books
options.D: Count her books aloud to confirm the number with the machine

questions.explanation: 亚历克斯解释问题源于她的水瓶放在秤上，并建议'下次确保秤上无物。'这强烈暗示她会遵循该建议。A是过度推断（她对解决方案感到宽慰，无回避机器的意图）。B是相关但未提及（她已成功扫描ID；新启示在于秤）。D是无关后续步骤（大声计数未提及或暗示）。

items.situation: two students discussing an issue with a campus sustainability program

conversation.speaker: Jessica
conversation.text: Hey Jason, you're in the Eco-Club, right? I wanted to ask about the reusable container program at the dining hall.

conversation.speaker: Jason
conversation.text: Yeah, definitely. What's up?

conversation.speaker: Jessica
conversation.text: Well, I borrowed one of the green containers for my salad yesterday, but I can't figure out where to return it. The sign at the dish drop-off is really confusing.

conversation.speaker: Jason
conversation.text: Oh, I know the one you mean. Actually, they moved the return bin last week. It's not by the regular dishes anymore.

conversation.speaker: Jessica
conversation.text: Really? So where is it now?

conversation.speaker: Jason
conversation.text: It's right next to the beverage station, by the wall. There's a new blue bin with a big logo on it.

conversation.speaker: Jessica
conversation.text: Huh, I must've walked right past it. I've been carrying this container around all day.

conversation.speaker: Jason
conversation.text: No worries. Just drop it there. And if the bin's full, you can also give it to any cashier—they'll scan it as returned so you don't get charged.

conversation.speaker: Jessica
conversation.text: Okay, good call. I'll head over there now. Thanks!

questions.stem: What is Jessica's problem?

options.A: She was charged for a reusable container she already returned.
options.B: She does not know where to return a borrowed reusable container.
options.C: The reusable container she borrowed is broken.
options.D: She cannot find the dining hall's beverage station.

questions.explanation: 杰西卡说：'我不知道该还到哪里。'A是错误细节（她尚未归还，被收费仅是潜在未来问题）。C是错误细节（容器状况未提及）。D是归因错误（她找不到归还箱，而非饮料站本身）。

questions.stem: What will Jessica most likely do if the blue return bin is full?

options.A: Take the container back to her dorm and try again tomorrow
options.B: Leave the container on top of the bin
options.C: Complain to the dining hall manager about the poor signage
options.D: Give the container to a cashier to scan as returned

questions.explanation: 贾森明确说明替代方案：'如果箱子满了，你也可以交给任何收银员——他们会扫描确认归还。'这是直接提及的计划。A是排除选项（她正前往并想归还）。B是无关后续步骤（未提及或暗示）。C是过度推断（她获得了所需信息，无投诉意图）。

items.situation: two students discussing a problem with the campus shuttle

conversation.speaker: Amy
conversation.text: Michael, you take the campus shuttle to the north dorms, right? Is it running on time today?

conversation.speaker: Michael
conversation.text: Actually, no. I just got the alert. The 3:15 shuttle is canceled.

conversation.speaker: Amy
conversation.text: Oh no! I have a study group at 3:30. How did you find out?

conversation.speaker: Michael
conversation.text: There's a text alert system. You can sign up on the transportation website. But don't worry, the next one is at 3:45.

conversation.speaker: Amy
conversation.text: 3:45? I'll be so late. Is walking faster?

conversation.speaker: Michael
conversation.text: It's about a twenty-minute walk from here. The shuttle, even at 3:45, would get you there by 3:55.

conversation.speaker: Amy
conversation.text: Hmm. So if I walk now, I'll only be about five minutes late. That's better than twenty-five.

conversation.speaker: Michael
conversation.text: Yeah, good call. I'd walk if I were you. And you should really sign up for those alerts.

conversation.speaker: Amy
conversation.text: I'll definitely do that. Thanks!

questions.stem: Why is Amy concerned about the shuttle?

options.A: She missed the shuttle and is waiting for the next one.
options.B: She thinks the shuttle route has changed.
options.C: Her shuttle has been canceled, and she has an appointment soon.
options.D: She does not know how to sign up for shuttle alerts.

questions.explanation: 迈克尔告诉她3:15的班车取消，她回应说3:30有学习小组。A是错误细节（她未错过；班车被取消）。B是错误话题（路线未讨论）。D是相关但未提及（她后来得知提醒，但这不是她的初始关切）。

questions.stem: What will Amy most likely do next?

options.A: Walk to her study group
options.B: Wait for the 3:45 shuttle
options.C: Cancel her study group meeting
options.D: Go to the transportation website immediately

questions.explanation: 艾米计算步行只会迟到五分钟，而等待班车将迟到二十五分钟，迈克尔同意，'我要是你就步行。'这表明她的决定。B是排除选项（她认为步行更好）。C是过度推断（她在想办法到达，而非取消）。D是归因错误（她说会注册提醒，但即时后续步骤是前往学习小组）。

items.situation: two students talking about a new vending machine or campus facility

conversation.speaker: Karen
conversation.text: Kevin, have you tried the new snack vending machine in the student center? The one that takes campus cash?

conversation.speaker: Kevin
conversation.text: I have, but I ran into a glitch. I selected a granola bar, paid with my phone, and then it got stuck in the spiral.

conversation.speaker: Karen
conversation.text: Wait, seriously? That's so frustrating. What did you do?

conversation.speaker: Kevin
conversation.text: Well, there's a small touchscreen on the front. I tapped 'Help' and it connected me via video call to a support person. They remotely released the spiral and it dropped right down.

conversation.speaker: Karen
conversation.text: Wow, that's actually pretty high-tech. Did you get a refund or anything?

conversation.speaker: Kevin
conversation.text: Not a refund, but they gave me a digital coupon for a free item next time. The whole thing took less than two minutes.

conversation.speaker: Karen
conversation.text: Hmm, so it's annoying but they fix it fast. I guess I'll still use it—the prices are better than the cafe.

conversation.speaker: Kevin
conversation.text: Exactly. Just be ready to tap that help screen if your snack gets hung up.

questions.stem: What are the students mainly discussing?

options.A: A problem with the student center's payment system
options.B: The high prices at the campus cafe
options.C: How to get a refund from a vending machine
options.D: A student's experience with a malfunctioning vending machine and its resolution

questions.explanation: 对话围绕凯文的零食卡住经历展开，讲述他如何通过帮助功能解决问题并获得优惠券。A选项过于局限（支付成功，问题是机械故障）。B选项偏离主题（仅作为对比简要提及）。C选项过于局限（他获得的是优惠券而非退款）。

questions.stem: What does Kevin imply about using the new vending machine in the future?

options.A: He will probably avoid it due to the risk of items getting stuck.
options.B: He will use it but knows how to quickly resolve any issues.
options.C: He will only use it if he has a digital coupon.
options.D: He will recommend the campus cafe instead.

questions.explanation: 凯文描述了高效的解决过程，并建议凯伦准备好使用帮助界面，暗示他懂得如何处理问题且未来可能会再次使用。A选项情境颠倒（他描述的是快速解决，并未建议不要使用）。C选项过度推断（优惠券是一次性补偿，并非未来使用条件）。D选项情境颠倒（他未推荐咖啡馆；凯伦指出自动售货机价格更优）。

items.situation: student checking with mail room about a missing package

conversation.speaker: Lisa
conversation.text: Hi, I got a notification that a package was delivered to the mail room yesterday, but I don't see it in my pickup box.

conversation.speaker: Brian
conversation.text: Okay, let me check. What's your last name and box number?

conversation.speaker: Lisa
conversation.text: It's Lopez, box 312.

conversation.speaker: Brian
conversation.text: Hmm. Lopez, 312... I see it here. Actually, this one's too big for the box. It's on the oversized shelf in the back.

conversation.speaker: Lisa
conversation.text: Oh, really? I didn't know you had a separate shelf for big items.

conversation.speaker: Brian
conversation.text: Yeah, we put a yellow slip in your box when that happens. You might've missed it.

conversation.speaker: Lisa
conversation.text: Ah, I probably just grabbed my letters and didn't look. Can I get it now?

conversation.speaker: Brian
conversation.text: Of course. I'll need to see your student ID, and I'll go grab it from the back. It'll just be a minute.

conversation.speaker: Lisa
conversation.text: Great, I've got my ID right here. Thanks!

questions.stem: Why was Lisa's package not in her pickup box?

options.A: It was too large and placed on a separate shelf.
options.B: It was delivered to the wrong mail room.
options.C: Someone else had already picked it up by mistake.
options.D: The delivery notification was sent in error.

questions.explanation: 布莱恩解释道：'这个包裹太大放不进信箱，放在后面的超大件货架上了。'B选项细节错误（包裹已正确送达收发室）。C选项相关但未提及（未说明或暗示）。D选项细节错误（通知是正确的；包裹就在那里）。

questions.stem: What will Lisa most likely do in the future when she gets a mail notification?

options.A: Call the mail room first to check the package size.
options.B: Wait an extra day before coming to pick up packages.
options.C: Look more carefully in her pickup box for any slips.
options.D: Ask a friend to pick up her packages for her.

questions.explanation: 布莱恩提到他们为超大件在信箱放了黄色通知单，丽莎说：'我可能只拿了信件没注意看。'这暗示她未来会更留意通知单。A选项过度推断（未提及或暗示要打电话）。B选项无关后续步骤（未建议等待）。D选项无关后续步骤（未提及）。

items.situation: two students debating whether to use a new campus app feature for finding study spots

conversation.speaker: Sarah
conversation.text: Hey Kevin, have you tried that new 'Spot Finder' feature in the campus app? It supposedly shows real-time availability for every study space on campus.

conversation.speaker: Kevin
conversation.text: I've seen the icon, but I didn't bother. Sounds like a gimmick. I usually just go to the library and hope for the best.

conversation.speaker: Sarah
conversation.text: Actually, I tested it this morning. It said the third-floor quiet zone had three open desks, and when I got there, it was spot on. No wasted time wandering around.

conversation.speaker: Kevin
conversation.text: Hmm, that's pretty impressive. But does it cover all the buildings? I often work in the student union or the science building basement.

conversation.speaker: Sarah
conversation.text: Yeah, it includes like twenty locations. Even the little nooks in the art building. You just filter by type—quiet zone, group study, or even café seating—and it shows a map.

conversation.speaker: Kevin
conversation.text: Okay, that might actually save me a ton of time. I wasted fifteen minutes yesterday walking between floors at the library because every room was full.

conversation.speaker: Sarah
conversation.text: Exactly! And it updates every minute. So if someone leaves, the spot frees up almost instantly.

conversation.speaker: Kevin
conversation.text: Alright, you've convinced me. I'll give it a shot tomorrow. Thanks for the tip!

questions.stem: What is the main topic of the conversation?

options.A: Sarah is encouraging Kevin to try a new app feature that helps find available study spaces.
options.B: Kevin is complaining about how crowded the library has become recently.
options.C: Sarah and Kevin are comparing different study locations on campus.
options.D: Kevin is asking Sarah for directions to a specific study room in the art building.

questions.explanation: 正确答案是A。对话的核心是Sarah向Kevin推荐并说服他使用新的校园App功能'Spot Finder'来寻找空余的学习位置。B选项错误，因为Kevin并没有抱怨图书馆拥挤，而是提到了他浪费时间的经历，但这只是讨论的背景。C选项错误，因为两人没有比较不同的学习地点，而是聚焦于一个工具。D选项错误，因为Kevin没有询问任何具体房间的方向。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Kevin's previous approach to finding a study spot?

options.A: He always used a different app to check availability.
options.B: He relied on asking friends for recommendations.
options.C: He would physically check multiple locations until he found a spot.
options.D: He never had trouble finding an empty desk before.

questions.explanation: 正确答案是C。Kevin提到'I usually just go to the library and hope for the best'以及'I wasted fifteen minutes yesterday walking between floors at the library because every room was full'，这表明他之前的方法是亲自去各个地方查看，直到找到空位。A选项错误，因为Kevin明确表示没有使用过这个功能。B选项错误，对话中没有提到他向朋友询问建议。D选项错误，他提到昨天浪费了15分钟，说明他确实遇到了困难。

items.situation: two students swapping music recommendations because one is bored of the same pop singers

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm so tired of my playlist. Every song sounds like the same three pop singers.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I went through that last semester. What kinds of things have you actually liked recently?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I guess songs with strong storytelling? Like a clear narrator and a few real-life details.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then you should try indie folk. There's a singer named Hana Bryant — she basically writes short stories. Her album from last fall, the one called 'Bus Stop Mornings,' is the place to start.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How does it compare to the regular pop stuff?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The arrangements are sparser — usually just guitar, sometimes piano. The whole point is that you focus on the lyrics.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That sounds like exactly what I need. I keep skipping tracks because they all blur together.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then I'd say start with the third track, 'Letters to Iowa.' If that one doesn't pull you in, you can give the rest a miss.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, third track. I'll listen on the bus tonight.

questions.stem: According to the man, what makes Hana Bryant's music different from the pop songs the woman is tired of?

options.A: The arrangements are sparser and meant to focus attention on the lyrics
options.B: The songs are produced entirely with electronic instruments
options.C: The albums are usually shorter than ten minutes
options.D: The singer rarely writes her own material

questions.explanation: 男生明说编曲简洁，多为吉他和钢琴，重点是让你专注歌词。A 正确。B 与原文相反，reversed_situation。C 没有依据，over_inference。D 与男生称她“写短篇小说”相反，wrong_detail。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Buy the entire album on physical CD this evening
options.B: Send Hana Bryant a personal email asking for new music
options.C: Listen to the third track from 'Bus Stop Mornings' on the bus tonight
options.D: Cancel her current pop music subscription completely

questions.explanation: 女生最后明确说今晚在公车上从第三首听起。C 正确。A 没提到买实体专辑，over_inference。B 完全没有这一情节，unrelated_next_step。D 是过度推断，over_inference。

items.situation: two friends discussing a movie one just saw — good twists but too long

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You saw 'Lighthouse Echo' last night, right? What did you think?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, I'm torn. The plot twists were really clever — I didn't see the ending coming at all.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's what everyone keeps saying online.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: But it was way too long. Almost three hours, and at least forty minutes in the middle could have been cut.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Like a slow stretch in the second act?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. There's this whole subplot about the brother that doesn't go anywhere. By the time the twist arrives, you've already lost some momentum.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did the audience seem bored?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: A few people behind me actually got up. Not walked out, but went to get popcorn around the ninety-minute mark.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm. I was thinking of seeing it tomorrow. Worth it for the twists?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: If you go in expecting a long ride, yes. Don't watch it tired.

questions.stem: What is the man's main complaint about the film?

options.A: The acting performances were uneven across the cast
options.B: The film was nearly three hours long, with a slow middle act that should have been cut
options.C: The opening twist was given away too early in the trailer
options.D: The soundtrack distracted from the film's most important scenes

questions.explanation: 男生明说时长接近三小时，中间部分本可以削减四十分钟。B 正确。A、C、D 男生都未提及，分别是 wrong_topic、over_inference、wrong_topic。

questions.stem: What does the man imply when he says "Don't watch it tired"?

options.A: The film should only be watched in a movie theater, not at home
options.B: The woman should plan to see the film with a large group of friends
options.C: The film contains scenes that are unsuitable for evening viewing
options.D: The film's length and slow middle will be harder to enjoy if she is already tired

questions.explanation: 男生暗示电影偏长、中段慢，疲倦时更难坚持。D 正确。A 没提观影方式，over_inference。B 没提到一群朋友，unrelated_next_step。C 是过度联想，over_inference。

items.situation: two students chatting about a gardening or art class they took

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did you finish the watercolor class at the community center?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Last week, actually. Six sessions, every Tuesday evening.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Was it worth signing up?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, more than I expected. The instructor focused on three basic skills: mixing colors, controlling water, and layering washes. By session four we were doing small landscapes.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did you need to bring your own materials?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The first session everything was provided — paper, brushes, paints. From session two on, you had to bring your own paper. Brushes and paints stayed in the room.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I was worried about the cost of supplies. How much did the paper come to?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I spent about eighteen dollars at the art store on a pad that lasted the whole course.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's manageable. I think I'll sign up for the next round.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: They open registration in two weeks. Don't wait — the evening section fills up fast.

questions.stem: What did students need to bring to the watercolor class starting from the second session?

options.A: A set of brushes and an apron
options.B: A small framed canvas for landscape work
options.C: Their own selection of dry paint pigments
options.D: Their own paper for the class exercises

questions.explanation: 男生说从第二节起需要自带纸张，画笔和颜料留在教室。D 正确。A、C 与原文相反，reversed_situation 或 wrong_detail。B 没有此环节，wrong_detail。

questions.stem: What does the man suggest the woman do?

options.A: Avoid the watercolor class because it requires expensive materials
options.B: Register early when the next round opens because evening sections fill quickly
options.C: Choose a daytime art class because evenings are reserved for advanced students
options.D: Take the class only after she completes a beginner sketching course

questions.explanation: 男生提醒别等，两周后开放报名，晚间段很快报满。B 正确。A 与他对课程的正面态度相反，reversed_situation。C、D 没有这种内容，unrelated_next_step。

items.situation: two roommates working out a fair chore or quiet-hours schedule

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Can we finally settle the chores and quiet-hours thing? I think we've been winging it for three weeks.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, agreed. The dishes have piled up twice already.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I was thinking we split it by day. You take dishes Monday and Wednesday, I take Tuesday and Thursday, and we trade Friday weekly.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Friday I'm usually out, so weekly trade works if you start. What about the trash?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Whoever's day it isn't, takes it down. That keeps it even.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Fair. And quiet hours? I have an 8 a.m. class three days a week, so anything past 11 is hard.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Eleven works for me, but I sometimes have group projects that go late. Could I use headphones after eleven if I'm on a call?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, as long as the conversation stays at a low volume. And the desk lamp instead of the overhead, if I'm sleeping.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Done. I'll write it up and stick it on the fridge.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Make sure the trash rule is in there. That's the one we always forget.

questions.stem: What are the two roommates mainly trying to do?

options.A: Agree on a written schedule for chores and quiet hours
options.B: Decide whether to move into a larger apartment next semester
options.C: Complain to the resident advisor about a noisy neighbor
options.D: Plan a weekend study session for their group project

questions.explanation: 整段对话围绕家务分工和安静时段安排，最后女生还说要写下来贴冰箱上。A 正确。B、C、D 都没有出现，都是 wrong_topic。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about how the roommates handle late-night noise?

options.A: They are unable to compromise and need a third party to mediate
options.B: They will rely on the resident advisor to enforce a strict 11 p.m. cutoff
options.C: They agree to flexibility past 11 p.m. as long as headphones and low-volume conversation are used
options.D: They have decided to ban all late-night calls or group work entirely

questions.explanation: 男生同意女生 11 点后可以用耳机低声通话，作为安静时段的灵活安排。C 正确。A、B、D 都与对话中达成的妥协相反，reversed_situation。

items.situation: two students chatting about a gardening or art class they took

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How's your urban-sketching elective going? I'm thinking of taking it next semester.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, it's been harder than I expected. The instructor pushes us to draw outside in twenty-minute bursts, and weather wrecks half the sessions.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh wow. Do you actually finish anything?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sometimes. But what surprised me is that the time pressure helps. Once you stop trying to be perfect, you actually start seeing things.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That sounds kind of inspiring, actually. Maybe I'll sign up after all.

questions.stem: Why does the woman approach the man?

options.A: To ask about his experience in an urban-sketching class
options.B: To borrow his sketching supplies for the weekend
options.C: To complain about the weather on campus
options.D: To recommend a different art elective to him

questions.explanation: 女生开场就问 "How's your urban-sketching elective going?"，表示她在考虑下学期选这门课。其他选项与对话开头主题无关。

questions.stem: What does the man imply about working under time pressure?

options.A: It made him quit the elective early.
options.B: It mainly causes him to make more mistakes.
options.C: It has unexpectedly improved how he observes scenes.
options.D: It only works when the weather is perfect.

questions.explanation: 男生说当你不再追求完美时，开始 "start seeing things"，暗示时间压力反而改善了他观察的方式。其他选项与他的语气或事实相反。

items.situation: two friends recommending a podcast or TV series to each other

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you tried that science podcast I mentioned, the one about everyday physics?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not yet. I've been hooked on a history series about ancient libraries. Episodes are about thirty minutes each.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that sounds great! Where did you find it?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's actually on the campus library's free streaming portal. You just log in with your student ID.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wait, seriously? I didn't even know we had that.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, it's pretty hidden. I'll send you the link tonight.

questions.stem: Where can the man's history series be accessed?

options.A: Through a paid podcast subscription
options.B: Through the campus library's free streaming portal
options.C: Through a commercial radio app
options.D: Through the science department's website

questions.explanation: 男生明确说节目在校园图书馆的免费流媒体平台上，用学生 ID 登录即可。其他来源与他的描述不符。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Cancel her existing podcast subscriptions
options.B: Recommend her physics podcast to the library
options.C: Wait for the man to send her the link
options.D: Look for the series through a paid streaming service

questions.explanation: 男生说今晚会把链接发给她，她最自然的下一步就是等他发链接。其他选项要么是对话未提及的行为，要么与谈话方向相反。

items.situation: two students talking about a pet or houseplant one is caring for

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How's that giant fern you adopted last month?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's actually thriving, surprisingly. I moved it next to my desk lamp and the new leaves came in within two weeks.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? I thought ferns hated bright light.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I thought so too. But this one's tropical, so it likes the warmth more than the brightness, I guess.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Huh, interesting. Mine keeps dropping leaves. Maybe I'm overwatering it.

questions.stem: What did the man do that helped his fern grow?

options.A: He moved it outside to a sunny balcony.
options.B: He repotted it in larger drainage soil.
options.C: He placed it near his desk lamp.
options.D: He started fertilizing it weekly.

questions.explanation: 男生说他把蕨类植物挪到 desk lamp 旁边，两周内长出新叶。其他选项是对话中未出现的具体动作。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the woman's fern?

options.A: It may be suffering from too much watering.
options.B: It is the same tropical species as the man's.
options.C: It has already died this semester.
options.D: It was originally a gift from the man.

questions.explanation: 女生说自己的蕨类在掉叶子，并猜测可能是浇水过多，所以最可能的推断就是 overwatering 是问题来源。其他选项无依据。

items.situation: two friends discussing a movie one just saw that surprised them

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I caught that documentary about urban farming last night. It honestly changed how I think about food.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Really? I'd assumed it was going to be more political.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I expected that too! But it focused on individual rooftop farmers in three different cities. Each story was super personal.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That sounds way better than the trailer suggested.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Exactly. The trailer made it look preachy, but the actual film is mostly conversations.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'll definitely give it a watch this weekend, then.

questions.stem: Why did the woman find the documentary effective?

options.A: It featured well-known political activists in detail.
options.B: It centered on personal stories of individual rooftop farmers.
options.C: It compared rooftop farming to traditional rural farming directly.
options.D: It included sweeping landscape footage of the countryside.

questions.explanation: 女生说纪录片聚焦三座城市的个别屋顶农夫的个人故事，所以这才是她觉得有效的原因。其他选项与她描述相反或无据。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the documentary's trailer?

options.A: It did not accurately represent the film's tone.
options.B: It was banned by several streaming services.
options.C: It was longer than the film itself.
options.D: It featured the same farmers as the documentary.

questions.explanation: 女生说 trailer 让纪录片看上去 preachy，但实际内容主要是对话，暗示预告片误导了观众对影片基调的判断。其他选项无依据。

items.situation: two friends coordinating a weekend hiking or beach trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Are we still on for the Cedar Ridge hike Saturday morning?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, definitely. But weather says rain after one, so we should aim for an early start.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Smart. I can pick you up at six-thirty if that works.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Six-thirty's perfect. Could you also bring an extra water bottle? Mine cracked last week.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sure, no problem. Anything else?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Just snacks, maybe. I'll bring the trail map.

questions.stem: What time will the woman pick up the man on Saturday?
questions.explanation: 女生提议六点半接他，男生说 perfect。其他时间或来自其他细节，或与对话事实不符。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the man's preparation?

options.A: He will replace his cracked water bottle himself.
options.B: He plans to navigate the hike using the trail map.
options.C: He will not need any snacks during the hike.
options.D: He intends to cancel the trip if it rains.

questions.explanation: 男生说自己会带 trail map，暗示他会用它在路上导航。其他选项与他的话相反或没有依据。

items.situation: two friends planning a birthday surprise for a classmate

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, are we still doing something for Daniela's birthday on Thursday?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I was thinking we could do a surprise at the study lounge after her seminar.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's a cute idea, but the lounge gets really loud around five. People are leaving the library. It'd ruin the moment.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm, fair point. What if we did the kitchenette in our dorm instead? It's quieter and there's a fridge for the cake.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Better. But Daniela never goes to your dorm. She'd be suspicious if you randomly invited her.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right. Okay, here's a thought — what if you ask her to come help you with the chemistry homework, and I just show up with the cake when she gets there?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's perfect, actually. She offered to help me last week, so it won't seem weird.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Great. I'll handle the cake and decorations. Can you message everyone else and tell them to be at your room by four-thirty?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sure. And I'll text Daniela tonight about the homework so she's expecting it.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to organize a surprise birthday celebration for their friend
options.B: How to finish their chemistry homework before Thursday evening
options.C: How to find a quiet place to study during the busy week
options.D: How to convince Daniela to move out of her current dorm

questions.explanation: 对话主要讨论如何为 Daniela 安排惊喜生日派对，A 正确。B 把化学作业当成主题（其实只是借口）；C 把安静地点过度概括；D 与对话无关。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: She will go pick up the birthday cake from the bakery
options.B: She will go finish her chemistry homework before the seminar
options.C: She will text Daniela about meeting to do homework together
options.D: She will go decorate her dorm room before everyone arrives

questions.explanation: 女生最后说她今晚会给 Daniela 发消息约一起做作业，C 正确。A 是男生负责；B 不是即将的行动；D 与对话中分工不符。

items.situation: two students comparing how they get to campus — bike, bus, or walk

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How's the new apartment? You moved off campus this semester, right?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, it's been great except for the commute. I'm trying to figure out the best way to get to my morning class.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How are you getting there now?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I've been taking the bus. It's reliable, but it takes thirty-five minutes because of all the stops.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you thought about biking? I bike every day. It's only twenty minutes if you take the riverside trail.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I don't have a bike, and honestly, on rainy days that sounds miserable.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Fair. The bus is probably your best bet for now, then. Walking would take forever from your place — at least forty-five minutes.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I tried walking once. Never again.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You could rent one of those campus share bikes by the month. Then on nice days, you switch to biking, and you keep the bus pass for bad weather.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, that's actually a smart compromise. I'll look into it.

questions.stem: How long does it take the man to reach campus by bus?

options.A: About twenty minutes each morning
options.B: About thirty-five minutes each morning
options.C: About forty-five minutes each morning
options.D: About one full hour each morning

questions.explanation: 男生说坐巴士要 35 分钟，B 正确。A 是女生骑车的时间；C 是步行时间；D 与对话无关。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next?

options.A: He will sell his new bus pass to a different student
options.B: He will move back to his old on-campus dormitory soon
options.C: He will buy a brand-new bike and a rain jacket today
options.D: He will look into renting a monthly campus share bike

questions.explanation: 男生最后说会去了解共享单车月租，D 正确。A 与对话无关；B 与对话相反；C 把买车与租车混淆，男生明确说不想买车。

items.situation: two friends figuring out a bus or train schedule for a day trip

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, so for the day trip to Riverton on Saturday, I'm looking at the transit schedule and it's confusing.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Why? What's the issue?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Well, the train leaves at seven-fifteen, which is great, but it only runs to the old Riverton station, which is forty-five minutes from the actual downtown.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh. And the bus?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: The bus goes straight to downtown but doesn't leave until nine-thirty. By the time we arrive, it'll be after eleven.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That cuts our day short. The galleries close at six.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Right. There's also a ride-share with another student club, but you have to commit by Wednesday and they're full.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Already? Okay. What if we just took the train and grabbed a taxi from the old station?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I checked. Taxis from there are expensive — like forty dollars each. That's almost double the train ticket.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hmm. You know what, the train is fine. Let's just split the taxi and treat ourselves. We'll still have most of the day for the galleries.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yeah, okay. Splitting it makes it manageable.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: Which gallery in Riverton they should visit first on Saturday
options.B: Whether to join a student club that runs a ride-share program
options.C: How to choose a transportation option for their Saturday day trip
options.D: Why the train schedule changed recently for the Riverton route

questions.explanation: 他们主要讨论如何选择周六的交通方式，C 正确。A 是旁支细节；B 只是被排除的选项之一；D 与对话无关。

questions.stem: What will the speakers most likely do for the trip?

options.A: They will take the early train and share a taxi together from the station
options.B: They will leave on the nine-thirty bus and cut their gallery visit short
options.C: They will wait for a spot to open up in the student club ride-share
options.D: They will cancel the trip and reschedule for the following weekend

questions.explanation: 男生提议坐火车再合租出租车，女生同意分摊费用，A 正确。B 与最终决定不符；C 已被排除（满了）；D 没有讨论取消。

items.situation: two students planning what cheese and food to buy for a dinner party

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Okay, for Friday's dinner party, I'm doing a cheese board. What should I get?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Definitely something soft, like brie. And then a harder one — sharp cheddar or maybe gouda.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good. I was thinking three cheeses total. What's the third one?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Something blue, if people are into it. But blue cheese is risky — not everyone likes it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yeah, maybe skip it. What about a goat cheese instead?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, that's safer. People generally like goat cheese. Get the kind with herbs on the outside.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Done. What else? I need crackers, obviously.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Crackers, and grapes. Grapes are essential. And get some honey for the goat cheese — trust me.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Honey, really? Okay, I'll trust you on that. What about meat? Should I add prosciutto?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yes, but only a small package. People go for the cheese first.

questions.stem: What does the man specifically recommend buying for the goat cheese?

options.A: A small jar of honey to serve with it
options.B: A wedge of imported gouda for the pairing
options.C: A package of dried fruit and salted nuts
options.D: A bottle of olive oil to brush on top

questions.explanation: 男生明确建议买蜂蜜搭配山羊奶酪，A 正确。B 把另一种奶酪误置；C 与对话无关；D 与对话无关。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about why the speakers decided against blue cheese?

options.A: Blue cheese was unavailable at the local grocery store
options.B: Blue cheese might not appeal to all of their guests
options.C: Blue cheese was too expensive for the small party budget
options.D: Blue cheese spoils more quickly than the other selections

questions.explanation: 男生说蓝奶酪有风险，并非人人喜欢，所以推断它可能不合所有客人口味，B 正确。A 与对话无关；C 与对话无关；D 与对话无关。

items.situation: two students chatting about a part-time job one just started at a factory

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I haven't seen you all week. How's the new job going?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, it's been an adjustment. I started that part-time job at the bottling plant on Monday.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: The bottling plant? I didn't think you'd take something like that.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, well, the pay is much better than the campus jobs. Sixteen an hour, plus a meal voucher each shift.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Wow, that's good. But what about the schedule? Doesn't it conflict with your classes?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's the part I'm still figuring out. They want me on the four-to-eight shift Monday through Thursday. My Tuesday lab usually runs until five.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oof. Can you switch labs?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I emailed the professor about moving to the Wednesday section. She hasn't replied yet, but I'm hoping she will.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: If not, can you switch shifts at work? Like, do Friday instead of Tuesday?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Maybe. I haven't asked. I was waiting to hear back from the professor first.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How the man is adjusting to his new part-time factory job
options.B: How the man should choose between two competing internships
options.C: Why the campus jobs typically pay less than off-campus jobs
options.D: How the woman could find part-time work at the same plant

questions.explanation: 对话主要讨论男生新工作的适应情况，特别是与课程的冲突，A 正确。B 偏离主题；C 把片段细节当主题；D 与对话无关。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do next regarding the schedule conflict?

options.A: He will quit the new job and apply to campus positions
options.B: He will wait for the professor's reply before contacting his manager
options.C: He will immediately swap his Tuesday shift to a Friday slot
options.D: He will drop the Tuesday lab and retake it next semester

questions.explanation: 男生说在听到教授回复前不打算和工厂谈，B 正确。A 与对话无关；C 与他说的“先等教授”相反；D 与对话无关。
